2026-03-11

Today I got the Neocities CLI running on my machine and pushed my site live! This lets me update my site without having to manually upload all my files (individually) from my computer. Neocities is good, but its web interface leaves a lot to be desired. This method won’t link my site inextricably to Github, since I can push direct to Neocities via the CLI. I could switch to Codeberg or something with minimal disruption, and that mobility is valuable to me.

There was a bit of difficulty in actually deploying the site. I was trying to follow the Astro blog tutorial, which only addresses Netlify. This confused me into thinking I needed to upload the entire Astro directory to Neocities. A friend helpfully directed me to the right part of the Astro docs that talks about properly deploying a statically generated site. It would be nice if the blog tutorial linked to this part of the docs. Astro has a lot of features, which means the docs can be kind of beastly to navigate.

TODO: I should submit a PR adding a line linking to the Deploy page from the blog tutorial. The Github actively encourages unsolicited PRs for typos, so. I’ll do that.

I still haven’t, uh, built the website yet. I’m learning as I go, but I want to have a way to log things and show my results while I learn. So the site is more or less the empty Astro site right now, with a “hello world” text. You can’t even read this blog post yet. Tomorrow I can proceed with the Astro blog tutorial to make this site look more like it should.
